introSection: 10 Inversion Roller Coaster was a steel roller coaster at Chimelong Paradise amusement park in Guangzhou, Guangdong China. Completed in 2006, 10 Inversion Roller Coaster was the second roller coaster in the world with ten inversions, after Colossus at Thorpe Park, of which it is an exact replica. The ride permanently closed in December 2025.

sections.title: Details
sections.content: There were 7 cars per train, arranged with 2 riders across in 2 rows, making a total of 28 riders per train.

fullText:
10 Inversion Roller Coaster was a steel roller coaster at Chimelong Paradise amusement park in Guangzhou, Guangdong China. Completed in 2006, 10 Inversion Roller Coaster was the second roller coaster in the world with ten inversions, after Colossus at Thorpe Park, of which it is an exact replica. The ride permanently closed in December 2025.

There were 7 cars per train, arranged with 2 riders across in 2 rows, making a total of 28 riders per train.
